methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
MightyFly is a Series A-stage autonomous drone company founded by Manal Habib. The target sector, Drones & Autonomous, contains eight private peers including Zipline, Joby Aviation, and Nuro, though no public comparables exist. The private comp universe remains sparse at MightyFly's stage and valuation tier, limiting robust peer-median analysis.

The valuation analysis applied one reliable method: Stage-Progression. Using the company's $10M total raised as a valuation proxy, a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A progression to subsequent funding rounds yields $20M. Peer-median analysis was not applicable; while five same-sector peers exist in the Drones & Autonomous universe, fewer than three comparables met strict stage and tier filters. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the specialized nature of autonomous systems.

The triangulated valuation range is $17M to $23M, with a median of $20M. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single methodology rather than multiple independent confirmation points. The primary uncertainty is the absence of closer peer data—comparable transaction multiples at this stage would either validate or materially shift this range. Secondary uncertainties include technology maturity, regulatory pathway clarity, and addressable market definition, none of which are quantified in available data. Investors should treat the $20M median as indicative pending deeper due diligence on operational metrics and market traction.
